label: Supabase
description: Connect to your Supabase project's managed Postgres database using a connection string or individual credentials.

form.title: Connect Supabase
form.subtitle: Link Anton to your Supabase project's Postgres database.

methods.label: Connection String
methods.description: Paste the full Postgres connection string from your Supabase project settings. Quickest way to get connected — one field, no assembly required.
methods.how_to:
## Get your Supabase connection string

1. Open [Supabase Dashboard](https://supabase.com/dashboard) and select your project.
2. Go to **Project Settings** (gear icon in the left sidebar) → **Database**.
3. Scroll to the **Connection string** section.
4. Choose the **URI** tab.
5. Copy the full string — it looks like `postgresql://postgres:[YOUR-PASSWORD]@db.<project-ref>.supabase.co:5432/postgres`.
6. If the password placeholder `[YOUR-PASSWORD]` appears, replace it with your database password (set when you created the project, or reset under **Database → Database Password**).
7. Paste the string into the field below.

### Notes

- Use the **Transaction** mode URI (port 6543) if you plan to run many short-lived queries; use the direct URI (port 5432) for long-running or migration workloads.
- If you're on the free plan and your project is paused, you'll need to unpause it in the dashboard first.

fields.name: connection_string
fields.label: Connection String
fields.description: Full Postgres URI from Project Settings → Database → Connection string → URI tab.

methods.label: Host / Port / Database / User / Password
methods.description: Enter your Supabase database credentials field by field. Useful if you need fine-grained control or a connection manager that doesn't accept raw URI strings.
methods.how_to:
## Get individual Supabase database credentials

1. Open [Supabase Dashboard](https://supabase.com/dashboard) and select your project.
2. Go to **Project Settings** → **Database**.
3. Scroll to the **Connection info** section — all individual fields are listed there.
4. Copy the values one by one:
   - **Host**: `db.<project-ref>.supabase.co`
   - **Port**: `5432` (direct) or `6543` (transaction pooler)
   - **Database name**: `postgres`
   - **User**: `postgres`
   - **Password**: your project database password
5. Fill in the fields below and click **Submit**.

### Notes

- If you've never set a database password, go to **Project Settings → Database → Database Password** and reset it.
- For SSL, Supabase requires `sslmode=require` — Anton enables this by default when connecting to Supabase hosts.

fields.name: host
fields.label: Host
fields.description: Found in Project Settings → Database → Connection info.

fields.name: port
fields.label: Port
fields.description: Use 5432 for a direct connection or 6543 for the transaction pooler.

fields.name: database
fields.label: Database Name
fields.description: The name of the database to connect to. Defaults to postgres on Supabase.

fields.name: user
fields.label: Database User
fields.description: The Postgres role to authenticate as. Defaults to postgres on Supabase.

fields.name: password
fields.label: Database Password
fields.description: Your project database password. Reset it in Project Settings → Database → Database Password if forgotten.

fields.name: ssl_mode
fields.label: SSL Mode
fields.description: Supabase requires SSL. Leave as require unless you have a specific reason to change it.

methods.label: REST API (Service Role Key)
methods.description: Use Supabase's auto-generated REST API with your service role key. Suitable for querying data via PostgREST without a direct Postgres connection.
methods.how_to:
## Get your Supabase service role key

1. Open [Supabase Dashboard](https://supabase.com/dashboard) and select your project.
2. Go to **Project Settings** → **API**.
3. Under **Project API keys**, copy the **service_role** key (labeled "secret").
4. Also copy the **Project URL** from the top of the same page — it looks like `https://<project-ref>.supabase.co`.
5. Paste both values into the fields below.

### Notes

- The **service_role** key bypasses Row Level Security (RLS). Keep it secret and never expose it in client-side code.
- The **anon** key is restricted by RLS and is NOT suitable for Anton — use the service_role key.
- This method queries data through PostgREST (Supabase's auto-generated REST layer) rather than a direct Postgres connection. Some complex SQL queries may not be expressible via the REST API; use the Connection String method for those.

fields.name: project_url
fields.label: Project URL
fields.description: Found in Project Settings → API → Project URL.

fields.name: service_role_key
fields.label: Service Role Key
fields.description: The secret service_role key from Project Settings → API → Project API keys. Bypasses Row Level Security — keep it safe.
